prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(initialize_combat_slot strike_member_alpha)
(assign_ability_token strike_member_alpha ability_token_assault)
(attach_ability_component strike_member_alpha ability_component_core)
(prime_slot_for_combo strike_member_alpha ability_component_core)
(attach_equipment_module strike_member_alpha equipment_module_sensor)
(stage_left_flank_primary strike_member_alpha left_flank_node_a ability_component_core)
(arm_strike_member_with_module strike_member_alpha left_flank_node_a equipment_module_sensor)
(initialize_combat_slot support_member_alpha)
(assign_ability_token support_member_alpha ability_token_shield)
(release_ability_component strike_member_alpha ability_component_core)
(attach_ability_component support_member_alpha ability_component_core)
(prime_slot_for_combo support_member_alpha ability_component_core)
(detach_equipment_module strike_member_alpha equipment_module_sensor)
(attach_equipment_module support_member_alpha equipment_module_sensor)
(stage_right_flank_primary support_member_alpha right_flank_node_a ability_component_core)
(arm_support_member_with_module support_member_alpha right_flank_node_a equipment_module_sensor)
(coordinate_flank_delivery strike_member_alpha support_member_alpha left_flank_node_a right_flank_node_a encounter_point_alpha)
(release_ability_component support_member_alpha ability_component_core)
(attach_ability_component strike_member_alpha ability_component_core)
(finalize_encounter_stage encounter_point_alpha strike_member_alpha ability_component_core)
(finalize_strike_member_combo strike_member_alpha encounter_point_alpha)
(finalize_support_member_combo support_member_alpha encounter_point_alpha)
(consume_recovery_resource strike_member_alpha recovery_pack_1 ability_component_core)
(redeploy_member_and_restock_token strike_member_alpha ability_token_assault recovery_pack_1)
(release_ability_component strike_member_alpha ability_component_core)
(attach_ability_component support_member_alpha ability_component_core)
(consume_recovery_resource support_member_alpha recovery_pack_1 ability_component_core)
(redeploy_support_member_and_restock_token support_member_alpha ability_token_shield recovery_pack_1)
(release_ability_component support_member_alpha ability_component_core)
(detach_equipment_module support_member_alpha equipment_module_sensor)
(initialize_combat_slot planner_agent_primary)
(assign_ability_token planner_agent_primary ability_token_assault)
(attach_ability_component planner_agent_primary ability_component_core)
(prime_slot_for_combo planner_agent_primary ability_component_core)
(apply_priority_marker_to_planner planner_agent_primary priority_marker_1 ability_component_core)
(attach_equipment_module planner_agent_primary equipment_module_sensor)
(arm_planner_priority_slot planner_agent_primary equipment_module_sensor)
(planner_assign_special_gadget_b planner_agent_primary special_gadget_b1)
(confirm_planner_priority_with_gadget_b planner_agent_primary special_gadget_b1)
(planner_accept_priority_and_signoff planner_agent_primary)
(planner_remove_special_gadget_b planner_agent_primary special_gadget_b1)
(consume_recovery_resource planner_agent_primary recovery_pack_1 ability_component_core)
(redeploy_planner_and_restock_token planner_agent_primary ability_token_assault recovery_pack_1)
(release_ability_component planner_agent_primary ability_component_core)
(detach_equipment_module planner_agent_primary equipment_module_sensor)
(initialize_combat_slot planner_agent_secondary)
(assign_ability_token planner_agent_secondary ability_token_shield)
(attach_ability_component planner_agent_secondary ability_component_core)
(prime_slot_for_combo planner_agent_secondary ability_component_core)
(install_tactical_module_on_encounter planner_agent_secondary tactical_module_beta encounter_point_alpha)
(arm_tactical_module_activation planner_agent_secondary tactical_module_beta encounter_point_alpha ability_component_core)
(planner_assign_special_gadget_a planner_agent_secondary special_gadget_a1)
(attach_equipment_module planner_agent_secondary equipment_module_sensor)
(prime_planner_module_for_activation planner_agent_secondary special_gadget_a1 equipment_module_sensor tactical_module_beta encounter_point_alpha)
(planner_assign_special_gadget_b planner_agent_secondary special_gadget_b1)
(prime_planner_with_special_gadget_b planner_agent_secondary special_gadget_b1 tactical_module_beta encounter_point_alpha)
(planner_finalize_without_synergy planner_agent_secondary)
(consume_recovery_resource planner_agent_secondary recovery_pack_1 ability_component_core)
(redeploy_planner_and_restock_token planner_agent_secondary ability_token_shield recovery_pack_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex, involving multiple units: strike_member_alpha, support_member_alpha, and two planner agents: planner_agent_primary and planner_agent_secondary. The goal is to get all four slots reconfigured: strike_member_alpha, support_member_alpha, planner_agent_primary, and planner_agent_secondary.


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task involves reconfiguring multiple units: strike_member_alpha, support_member_alpha, and two planner agents, planner_agent_primary and planner_agent_secondary.  
- The goal is to ensure all four slots—strike_member_alpha, support_member_alpha, planner_agent_primary, and planner_agent_secondary—are properly reconfigured.  
- The complexity arises from managing multiple interrelated components in the reconfiguration process.

## Examining Slot Initialization  
- The process begins with initializing strike_member_alpha, support_member_alpha, planner_agent_primary, and planner_agent_secondary for reconfiguration.  
- The action initialize_combat_slot is used to set up these slots, with ability tokens like assault, shield, and heal assigned, though only three tokens exist for four slots.  

## Analyzing Token and Resource Constraints  
- Limited resources include one recovery_pack_1, one consumable_kit_alpha, and one ability_component_core, impacting the reconfiguration process.  
- Tokens and equipment, such as one sensor and two tactical options (alpha and beta), must be managed carefully due to shortages.  

## Exploring Reconfiguration Process  
- Reconfiguring each slot requires cycling through recovery and redeploy actions, with redeploy making recovery resources reusable.  
- Combo execution, necessary for slot reconfiguration, involves finalize_strike_member_combo and planner_signoff, linked to encounter engagement and resource consumption.  

## Investigating Planner Roles  
- Both planner_agent_primary and planner_agent_secondary are linked to the same members and encounter alpha, with primary using priority_marker_1 and secondary linked to priority_marker_2.  
- Priority_marker_2 may not be available initially, suggesting planner paths could differ, with both having tactical modules (alpha for primary, beta for secondary).

## Continuing Reconfiguration Process  
- Strike and support units require staging, engagement, and finalization to execute combos, with similar processes for both left and right flanks.  
- Planners, also treated as combat_unit_slots, need initialization, token assignment, component binding, and priming for their own combo execution.  

## Exploring Planner Paths  
- Planners can follow a priority path, involving applying priority markers, arming, and confirming with gadgets, leading to signoff and combo execution without needing encounter engagement.  
- Alternatively, planners can use a tactical path, installing modules on the encounter after engagement and finalization, with complex steps involving priming, arming, and synergy for combo execution.  

## Analyzing Planner Strategy  
- With two planners and one encounter, one might use the shorter priority path while the other uses the tactical path, given both have tactical modules and encounter access.  
- Priority actions focus on internal slot preparation, while tactical actions rely on encounter conditions, suggesting flexibility in planner roles.

## Examining Tactical Path Requirements  
- Tactical path installation on the encounter requires it to be engaged and finalized first.  
- Priming and confirming in the tactical path necessitate both special_a and special_b gadget links, with equipped modules also required.  

## Analyzing Priority Path Needs  
- Priority path uses priority markers, gadget_b, and equipped modules, but does not require special_a.  
- Both paths need gadget_b for confirmation, creating a resource conflict since only one gadget_b is available.  

## Investigating Gadget Reusability  
- Gadgets can be removed and reassigned between planners using actions like planner_remove_special_gadget_b, enabling reuse.  
- This reusability, along with restocked tokens and recovery via redeploy, supports both planners achieving their reconfiguration goals.  

## Coordinating Planner Strategies  
- Primary planner can use the priority path with priority_marker_1, while secondary, lacking priority_marker_2, may take the tactical path using tac beta.  
- Component binding for strike and planners must be coordinated, ensuring encounter engagement and finalization align with tactical needs.

## Managing Encounter Finalization  
- Encounter engagement is set by loading and opening, establishing left and right links, preparing for further actions.  
- Finalizing the encounter stage requires the strike ability_component_core to be bound, necessitating release from support and reattachment to strike.  

## Finalizing Member Combos  
- Once encounter is finalized, member combos can be completed, setting combo_exec for both strike and support members.  
- This process involves ensuring stage flags and payload staging are in place, aligning with encounter conditions.  

## Reconfiguring Members  
- Members need the ability_component_core bound to consume recovery resources, entering recovery and preparing for redeployment.  
- Redeployment restocks tokens like assault and shield, marking members as reconfigured and making recovery resources available again.  

## Coordinating Component Usage  
- Ability components must be released from one member (e.g., strike) and attached to another (e.g., support) to facilitate recovery and reconfiguration.  
- This coordination ensures all members achieve combo execution and reconfiguration, with tokens like heal remaining unused.

## Preparing Tactical Module  
- Install tactical_module_beta on encounter_point_alpha for planner_agent_secondary, ensuring slot is primed and tactical module is available.  
- Arm the tactical module using ability_component_core, confirming no encounter coverage, which sets the planner_module_armed status.  

## Priming Planner Module  
- Attach equipment_module_sensor and special_gadget_a1 to planner_agent_secondary to meet priming requirements, ensuring special links and equipped status.  
- Prime the planner module for activation, using tactical_module_beta and no right coverage, setting planner_module_primed status.  

## Managing Gadget Availability  
- Remove special_gadget_b1 from planner_agent_primary after use to make it available for planner_agent_secondary, ensuring gadget reusability.  
- Assign special_gadget_b1 to planner_agent_secondary, enabling confirmation with no coverage, aligning with tactical needs.

## Initializing Combat Slots  
- Start by initializing combat slots for strike_member_alpha and support_member_alpha sequentially.  
- Assign ability tokens (assault for strike, shield for support) to prepare each member for action.  

## Configuring Member Abilities  
- Attach ability_component_core to strike_member_alpha, prime for combo, and stage left flank with equipment_module_sensor.  
- After strike is ready, release ability_component_core from strike, attach to support_member_alpha, and stage right flank.  

## Managing Equipment Transfer  
- Detach equipment_module_sensor from strike_member_alpha once support is prepped, then attach to support_member_alpha.  
- Ensure both members are armed and ready before coordinating flank delivery at encounter_point_alpha.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
